title: Coat of arms of Sweden

infobox.value: Azure, quartered by a cross or with outbent arms, and an inescutcheon containing the dynastic arms of the Royal House. In the first and fourth fields three open crowns Or, placed two above one. In the second and third fields three sinisterbendwise streams argent, a lion crowned with an open crown or armed gules. The inescutcheon is party per pale the arms for the House of Vasa (Bendwise azure, argent and gules, a vasa (sheaf of wheat) Or); and the House of Bernadotte (Azure, issuant from a wavy base a bridge with three arches and two towers embattled argent, in honour point an eagle regardant with wings inverted resting on thunderbolts Or, and in chief the Big Dipper constellation of the same).
two lions regardant, crowned and with forked tails (queue fourchée) Or armed gules, standing on a compartment Or
All surrounded by ermine mantling, crowned with a royal crown and tied up with tasseladorned strings Or
| | | | |  | | [House of Bjälbo](/wiki/House_of_Bj%C3%A4lbo "House of Bjälbo") | | | | | |  | | [House of Bernadotte](/wiki/House_of_Bernadotte "House of Bernadotte") | | | | |

infobox.key: coat of arms of the Kingdom of Sweden
infobox.value: ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Sveriges riksvapen*) is the [arms of dominion](/wiki/Arms_of_dominion "Arms of dominion") of the [Swedish monarch](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Sweden "Monarchy of Sweden"). It has a greater and a lesser version. The shield displays the "Three Crowns of Sweden" quartering the "Lion of Bjälbo", with an inescutcheon overall of the House of Vasa impaling the House of Bernadotte.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/95/HMS_Gustav_V.jpg/250px-HMS_Gustav_V.jpg)](/wiki/File:HMS_Gustav_V.jpg)

Arms adorning the stern of [HSwMS Gustaf V](/wiki/HSwMS_Gustaf_V "HSwMS Gustaf V")

The **coat of arms of the Kingdom of Sweden** ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *Sveriges riksvapen*) is the [arms of dominion](/wiki/Arms_of_dominion "Arms of dominion") of the [Swedish monarch](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Sweden "Monarchy of Sweden"). It has a greater and a lesser version. The shield displays the "Three Crowns of Sweden" quartering the "Lion of Bjälbo", with an inescutcheon overall of the House of Vasa impaling the House of Bernadotte.

## Regulated usage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Sweden&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Regulated usage")]

The usage of the coats of arms is regulated by Swedish Law, [Act](/wiki/Swedish_Code_of_Statutes "Swedish Code of Statutes") [1970:498](https://rkrattsbaser.gov.se/sfst?bet=1970:498) which states (in unofficial translation) that "in commercial activities, the coats of arms, the flag or other official insignia of Sweden may not be used in a trademark or other insignias for products or services without proper authorisation. This includes any mark or text referring to the Swedish State which this can give the commercial mark a sign of official endorsement. This includes municipal coats of arms which are registered."

Any representation consisting of three crowns ordered two above one are considered to be the lesser coat of arms, and its usage is therefore restricted by law 1970:498.

## Variants

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Sweden&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Variants")]

The arms of Sweden were first formally codified by law in 1908.[[2]](#cite_note-2) This law also formally codifies the differences between the "greater" and "lesser" arms. The present law prescribing the two arms dates from 1982.

### Greater version

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Sweden&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Greater version")]

The greater coat of arms is blazoned in Swedish law as follows:

> *A shield [azure](/wiki/Azure_(heraldry) "Azure (heraldry)"), quartered by a cross [Or](/wiki/Or_(heraldry) "Or (heraldry)") with outbent arms, and an [inescutcheon](/wiki/Escutcheon_(heraldry) "Escutcheon (heraldry)") containing the dynastic arms of the Royal House. In the first and fourth fields three [open crowns](/wiki/Crown_(heraldry) "Crown (heraldry)") Or, placed two above one. In the second and third fields three sinisterbendwise streams [argent](/wiki/Argent "Argent"), a [lion](/wiki/Lion_(heraldry) "Lion (heraldry)") crowned with an open crown Or armed [gules](/wiki/Gules "Gules"). The inescutcheon is [party per pale](/wiki/Division_of_the_field "Division of the field") the arms for the [House of Vasa](/wiki/House_of_Vasa "House of Vasa") (Bendwise azure, argent and gules, a* vasa *Or); and the [House of Bernadotte](/wiki/House_of_Bernadotte "House of Bernadotte") (Azure, issuant from a wavy base a bridge with three arches and two towers embattled argent, in honor point an [eagle regardant with wings inverted resting on thunderbolts](/wiki/Eagle_of_Zeus "Eagle of Zeus") Or, and in chief the Big Dipper constellation of the same). The main shield is crowned by a royal crown and surrounded by the insignia of the [Order of the Seraphim](/wiki/Order_of_the_Seraphim "Order of the Seraphim"). [Supported](/wiki/Supporters "Supporters") by two lions regardant, crowned and with forked tails Or armed gules, standing on a [compartment](/wiki/Compartment_(heraldry) "Compartment (heraldry)") Or. All surrounded by [purpure](/wiki/Purpure_(heraldry) "Purpure (heraldry)") mantling doubled [ermine](/wiki/Ermine_(heraldry) "Ermine (heraldry)"), crowned with a royal crown and tied up with tasseled strings Or.*[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The greater arms may also be displayed only with the crowned escutcheon. While the arms have undergone significant changes over the years, such as changing the inescutcheon with the ruling dynasty, they are based on arms created by King [Charles VIII](/wiki/Charles_VIII_of_Sweden "Charles VIII of Sweden") in 1448.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The [escutcheon](/wiki/Escutcheon_(heraldry) "Escutcheon (heraldry)") used in the greater blazon has in total five elements: 4 [quarterings](/wiki/Quartering_(heraldry) "Quartering (heraldry)") on the main escutcheon (two coats of arms duplicated), and three coat of arms incorporated into an escutcheon of pretense. However, Bernadotte never used any stars in the arms of Pontecorvo (neither as Prince of Pontecorvo, nor as King of Sweden and Norway). The stars were introduced as an element in the royal coat of arms in the 19th century,[[5]](#cite_note-5) chosen as a symbol of Sweden's eternal existence, as in the poem by [Esaias Tegnér](/wiki/Esaias_Tegn%C3%A9r "Esaias Tegnér"):

> As long as [Charles's Wain](/wiki/Charles%27s_Wain "Charles's Wain") still turns,  
> Its golden wheels around the Northern zone,  
> As long as the land still produces iron and heroes,  
> Intact shall stand the ancient Swedish throne.

This symbol became especially popular through its allusion to the name that had been borne by so many famous Swedish kings. The [Big Dipper](/wiki/Big_Dipper "Big Dipper"), or as it is called in Swedish, *Karlavagnen* (Charles' Wain), adds a Swedish accent to the Bernadotte dynastic coat of arms much in the same way as do the Vasa arms.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

|  | |  | |

The arms are supported by two lions with forked tails (*queue fourchée*), facing away from the shield and crowned with Royal Crowns. For centuries, the lion has been an important element in Swedish heraldry and especially for the State Coat of Arms. The shield may be surmounted by the Collar of the [Order of Seraphim](/wiki/Order_of_Seraphim "Order of Seraphim"), the foremost order in Sweden, and the highest honour the Swedish state can bestow on an individual.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Besides being the official national coat of arms, the greater coat of arms is also the personal coat of arms of the king, and as such he can decree its use as a personal coat of arms by other members of the [Royal House](/wiki/House_of_Bernadotte "House of Bernadotte"), with the alterations and additions decided by him.

[Blazon](/wiki/Blazon "Blazon"): "The greater state arms consist of a head shield azure, quartered by a cross or with outbent arms, and an [inescutcheon](/wiki/Inescutcheon "Inescutcheon") containing the dynastic arms of the Royal House.

In the first and fourth fields three coronets or, placed two above one. In the second and third fields three sinisterbendwise streams argent, a lion crowned with an open crown or with armaments gules. The inescutcheon is party per pale the arms for the [House of Vasa](/wiki/House_of_Vasa "House of Vasa") and the [House of Bernadotte](/wiki/House_of_Bernadotte "House of Bernadotte"). The main shield is crowned by a royal crown and surrounded by the insignia of the [Order of the Seraphim](/wiki/Order_of_the_Seraphim "Order of the Seraphim"). Supported by two lions regardant or crowned, with parted tails and armaments gules, standing on a postament. All surrounded by hermine mantling crowned with a royal crown and tied up with tasseled strings or."

### Lesser version

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coat_of_arms_of_Sweden&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Lesser version")]

The lesser coat of arms is mainly used by the [Government of Sweden](/wiki/Government_of_Sweden "Government of Sweden") and subordinate [State](/wiki/State_(Sweden) "State (Sweden)") authorities. As such it may be joined by insignias symbolising the activity of individual government agencies, following approval by the State Board of Heraldry. It is, for instance, embroidered on all [Swedish police](/wiki/Swedish_police "Swedish police") uniforms and in various coats of arms of the [Swedish Armed Forces](/wiki/Swedish_Armed_Forces "Swedish Armed Forces"), and is displayed on Sweden's [passports](/wiki/Swedish_passport "Swedish passport") and [embassies](/wiki/List_of_diplomatic_missions_of_Sweden "List of diplomatic missions of Sweden").

Blazon: "Azure, with three coronets Or, ordered two above one." Crowned with a royal crown. The shield may also be surrounded by the insignias of the [Order of the Seraphim](/wiki/Order_of_the_Seraphim "Order of the Seraphim")."

1. **[^](#cite_ref-HT_1-0)** Loÿe, Georges de (1986). "Om ursprunget till det svenska riksvapnet Tre Kronor". *Heraldisk Tidsskrift* (in Swedish). **1986** (6): 54, 155–174 : ill. [ISSN](/wiki/ISSN_(identifier) "ISSN (identifier)") [0440-6966](https://search.worldcat.org/issn/0440-6966). 0440-6966.

3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** Original text of Swedish statute 1982:268, 2 §, states: *Stora riksvapnet utgörs av en blå huvudsköld, kvadrerad genom ett kors av guld med utböjda armar, samt en hjärtsköld som innehåller det kungliga husets dynastivapen. Huvudsköldens första och fjärde fält innehåller tre öppna kronor av guld, ordnade två över en. Huvudsköldens andra och tredje fält innehåller tre ginbalksvis gående strömmar av silver, överlagda med ett upprest, med öppen krona krönt lejon av guld med röd tunga samt röda tänder och klor. Hjärtskölden är kluven. Första fältet innehåller Vasaättens vapen: ett i blått, silver och rött styckat fält, belagt med en vase av guld. Andra fältet innehåller ätten Bernadottes vapen: i blått fält en ur vatten uppskjutande bro med tre valv och två krenelerade torn, allt av silver, däröver en örn av guld med vänstervänt huvud och sänkta vingar gripande om en åskvigg av guld samt överst Karlavagnens stjärnbild av guld. Huvudskölden är krönt med en kunglig krona och omges av Serafimer ordens insignier. Sköldhållare är två tillbakaseende, med kunglig krona krönta lejon med kluvna svansar samt röda tungor, tänder och klor. Lejonen står på ett postament av guld. Det hela omges av en med kunglig krona krönt hermelinsfodrad vapenmantel av purpur med frans av guld och uppknuten med tofsprydda snören av guld. Stora riksvapnet får brukas även utan ordensinsignier, sköldhållare, postament eller vapenmantel.*
